title: Eremophila homoplastica

The family Scrophulariaceae is an ancient group of flowering plants often referred to as the figwort family. Eremophila homoplastica is native to Western Australia, where it grows in the wild. The exact classification of Eremophila homoplastica may be subject to change as new information becomes available. intro:
There is no information available on a plant called "Eremophila homoplastica". However, I found information on a plant called "Eremophila" or "Emu Bush". 

Eremophila, also known as Emu Bush, is a genus of flowering plants native to Australia, known for their vibrant flowers and medicinal properties. The Eremophila plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eremophila include iridoids, phenylethanoid glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Common herbal preparations made with Eremophila include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
